title: Trump says ceasefire is intact after U.S. and Iran exchange fire
description: The burst of violence cast fresh doubt on efforts by Washington and Tehran to reach a negotiated settlement that would end hostilities.

title: As Marcomentum sweeps the internet, Rubio, White House swear off 2028 talk
description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio has won over some former critics while Vice President JD Vance struggles with Trump’s shadow.

title: Judge rules DOGE’s cuts to humanities grants were unconstitutional
description: A federal judge said more than $100 million in cuts to NEH grants were discriminatory, ruling in a case that revealed the inner workings of DOGE.

title: Aides cite friendly meeting as Rubio aims to mend U.S. ties with Pope Leo
description: Relations between the United States and the Vatican are at a low point over President Donald Trump’s attacks on Leo, who is a leading critic of the war in Iran.

title: Tennessee lawmakers approve new House map to eliminate Democratic-held seat
description: Republicans in the state could hold a 9-0 advantage in the U.S. House with their new map, after the Supreme Court weakened the Voting Rights Act last week.

title: Trump pledged to free Jimmy Lai. Now U.S. lawmakers want action at China summit.
description: The president previously committed to helping the 78-year-old Lai, a symbol of Hong Kong’s pro-democracy movement who has been imprisoned since 2020.

title: Tennessee joins southern push to redistricting in favor of the GOP
description: Over protests in the capitol, Tennessee lawmakers joined the rush of southern Republicans to redraw congressional voting maps now that protections for minority voting power have been weakened.

title: With legal briefs in, Supreme Court weighs telehealth access for the abortion pill
description: After an appeals court tried to end telemedicine access to mifepristone, one of the two pills used in a medication abortion, the Supreme Court stepped in. Here's what's happened and what's to come.

title: Karoline Leavitt announces birth of baby girl and will take maternity leave
description: Viviana was born May 1, making Leavitt the first White House press secretary to give birth while in the job. She has not yet set a date to return to work.

title: U.S. intelligence says Iran can outlast Trump’s Hormuz blockade for months
description: A confidential intelligence community assessment delivered to the White House also finds that Iran retains a substantial missile and drone arsenal.

title: DHS blames funding lapse for shutdown of internal detention oversight
description: The office investigates detainee deaths and access to medical care, among other issues. It's being wound down, even as the number of detention deaths and length of detention stays has grown.

title: A judge unsealed Epstein's purported 2019 suicide note. More documents could follow
description: At the request of the New York Times, a judge unsealed a goodbye note that Jeffrey Epstein's former cellmate says he found after the convicted sex offender's first suicide attempt in July 2019.

title: Tennessee Republicans pass a map to break up the state's lone Democratic House seat
description: Tennessee Republicans' map would crack Shelby County — home to majority-Black Memphis — into three different districts, in an effort to eliminate the state's lone remaining Democratic-held seat.

title: Democrats see Senate control within reach — but some big hurdles remain
description: The political terrain remains forbidding, but the president’s crumbling approval ratings and the Iran war’s weight on the economy offer the party an opening.

title: Undeterred by polls and courts, Trump pushes ballroom, honors donors
description: Amid the war with Iran, surging gas prices and backlash to his immigration policies, the president continues to dedicate extensive time to his signature project.

title: Federal workers had their annual award show. Many were too afraid to show up.
description: A year after the U.S. DOGE Service dramatically slashed the civil service, government workers are still spooked about getting on the Trump administration’s bad side.

title: Rep. Chris Deluzio, D-Pa, on proposed legislation to cut off funding for Iran war
description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Democratic Congressman Chris Deluzio of Pennsylvania, one of 18 lawmakers backing new legislation that would cut off funding for the war in Iran.

title: Psychiatrists say RFK Jr.'s take on SSRIs is an 'oversimplification' of the problem
description: The American Psychiatric Association says too few patients can access comprehensive mental health care in the United States. It welcomes new investments in improving access to evidence-based care.

title: Border czar promises 'mass deportations are coming' to fulfill Trump's promises
description: The remarks contrast with Border Czar Tom Homan's softer messaging earlier this year, after two U.S. citizens were killed by immigration officials in Minneapolis.

title: In a new poll, Americans voice broad bipartisan support for age caps in Congress
description: The vast majority of Americans — 8 in 10 — say there should be age caps for members of Congress, as well as term limits, according to the latest NPR/PBS News/Marist poll.

title: Campaign staffers tell NPR they make 'thousands' betting on their own candidates
description: Campaign staffers are turning private polling data into personal paydays. They describe the election prediction market as a "Wild West" for staffers.

title: Judge denies request to return 2020 ballots seized from Fulton County, Georgia
description: The FBI’s seizure of Fulton County’s voting materials has stoked alarm among election officials and democracy advocates.

title: Court releases purported suicide note from Jeffrey Epstein
description: The note, which was discovered by Epstein’s former cellmate, had been under seal for years. No court or investigative agency has vouched for its authenticity.

title: FBI raids business of state Sen. L. Louise Lucas, a key Virginia Democrat
description: The offices of state Sen. L. Louise Lucas, a Democrat, as well as a cannabis dispensary she co-owns were raided. The exact nature and targets of the probe remain unclear.

title: Lutnick testifies before congressional panel investigating Epstein
description: The commerce secretary participated in a voluntary interview with the House Oversight Committee to explain his ties to the late financier and convicted sex offender.

title: Sen. Susan Collins discloses ‘essential tremor’ diagnosis to explain shaking
description: The Maine senator who is up for reelection this year described her condition as ‘extremely common’ with ‘absolutely no impact on my ability to do my job.’

title: Justice Department launches civil rights probe into prosecutor in Virginia
description: Fairfax County Commonwealth’s Attorney Steve Descano (D) has been under attack from Republicans over his handling of cases involving immigrant defendants.

title: Trump hosts UFC fighters in Oval Office, touts Iran progress
description: The unusual White House scene highlighted President Donald Trump’s diverse set of priorities as he juggles a war with event planning.

title: Trump lashes out at Pope Leo again ahead of Rubio trip to Rome
description: The criticism of the pontiff’s stance on Iran highlighted what Vatican officials have described as an unprecedented low in relations with the United States.

title: Poll finds broad rejection of religion-related messages from Trump, Hegseth
description: Americans rate Pope Leo positively but are deeply critical of the president’s social media post that appeared to depict him as Jesus, the Post-ABC-Ipsos poll found.

title: After defying Trump, a Republican lawmaker hangs on by a thread
description: Republicans in Indiana’s Senate tested voters’ tolerance for defying the president. Most lost in primaries amid a wave of threats, campaign cash and popular backlash.

title: The pro-Israel political consensus is collapsing in both parties
description: On the campaign trail and elsewhere, the alliance has gone from a bipartisan consensus to a subject of fierce debate among Republicans and Democrats alike.

title: 4 takeaways from the Indiana and Ohio primaries
description: Primaries in Indiana and Ohio reinforced President Donald Trump’s power in the GOP and set the stakes for several top-tier midterm races.
